summary: solidity-deploy-guard exfiltrates host and environment data to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: Package contains an install-time/lifecycle payload in package.json (line 30) and a scanner.js module that together collect host metadata and POST it to a hardcoded attacker-controlled endpoint at https://ddjidd564.github.io. The payload combines require('child_process'), require('os'), require('https'), and require('fs') to run system commands (execSync, whoami), read os.hostname() and process.env, walk the filesystem with fs.existsSync, and ship the results out via https.request POST and curl invocations. The destination is a personal GitHub Pages site unrelated to any documented Solidity tooling publisher, with no integrity check or configurability. There is no legitimate reason for a 'deploy guard' utility to enumerate the host and beacon system intel to a third-party github.io site.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
